title: Delicate Yeasty Turkish Meat Cig Kofte
description: A traditional Turkish dish made with ground meat and spices, featuring a yeasty and aerated texture, and served with a squeeze of unwaxed lemon juice, this Whole30-friendly recipe is perfect for a healthy and energizing meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
In a small bowl, mix the ferment-jang and lemon juice until well combined.
Add the ferment-jang mixture to the meat mixture and mix until just combined.
In a separate bowl, proof the yeast by mixing it with the water and letting it sit for 5 minutes.
Add the yeast mixture to the meat mixture and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a cylinder.
Grill the cig kofte for 4-5 minutes per side, or until they are cooked through and slightly charred.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
